WASHINGTON, Dec. ia car
famine has stirred up the shippers
throughout the country, and walls
are coming to the interstate COM
merce commission from the four
corners of the map. Everything is
tied up. Cotton in the South, wheat
in the Northwest, fruit in the
Southwest and general merchan'
disc everywhere.


But the worst trouble is reported
from the farmers of the great
wheat region of the Dakotas, Min.
nesota, Iowa and Wisconsin. The
precious wheat is lying in great
mounds covered with snow, becom
ing saturated with water and GER
tain to be ruined.


Appeals for cars to move ii are
vain. The local elevators are
practically empty. The cars, mean
time. are lying in long rows on the
sidings in the great centers like
Chicago, Pittsburg. Kansas City
and Seattle.


Farmers are frantic. They can
not get money to pay their harvest
ing bills, because they cannot get
their wheat to market. The wheat
which ought to be worth so much
gold, is not good for security unit
It is in an elevator and represented
by warehouse receipt.


WHO is responsible? That Is
what the interstate commerce com
mission Is determined to find out
Hearings have begun in the large
cities of the Middle West. Com
missioner Lane is chiefly interest
ed in this affair.


Is it a railroad conspiracy? I:
there a concerted eort to bring tht
